Narrow fixes covered public support-hotline bait, obfuscated VAT/tax/passport phone-shaped fragments, dotted-date and filing-reference phone bait, role/placeholder emails, template passport values, EU registry/ISIN/LEI/tax numeric fragments, log-id amount fragments, placeholder country-code phones, and negated MAE/contingent-MNPI language. Full EU member-state national-ID breadth and broader semantic GDPR special-category coverage remain ideal-tier gaps.

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: contemplated acquisition of Heliovia Wind GmbH (private)
If we observe abnormal price/volume suggesting a leak
no pre-briefing of analysts or key investors is permitted
Subject: MAR, GDPR (incl. Art 9), cross-border transfer, and CSRD/SFDR workflows for Q2 trading update and contemplated acquisition of Heliovia Wind GmbH (private).

The contemplated binding SPA targeted for 14 June 2026 (after market close on Bourse de Montciel, Segment Réglementé) together with preliminary unaudited Q2 revenue variance >12% versus guidance constitute information privilégiée under Art 7 MAR; we propose a delay under Art 17(4) MAR until SPA signing, maintain and update insider lists (Art 18 MAR), and prepare a communiqué de presse for immediate release if a leak is suspected.

Cross-border data transfer: reviewers in our Norlandia subsidiary (non-EEA) require access; implement 2021 SCCs, complete a Transfer Impact Assessment, pseudonymize HR data where feasible, and restrict downloads; processors must be bound by EU-standard confidentiality and breach-notification terms.

Communications: all investor interactions must cite only regulated information; any slide decks require Compliance clearance and should be released after market close with simultaneous OAM filing, aligning with Bourse de Montciel timing guidance.

Subject: MAR, GDPR (incl. Art 9), cross-border transfer, and CSRD/SFDR workflows for Q2 trading update and contemplated acquisition of Heliovia Wind GmbH (private).
The contemplated binding SPA targeted for 14 June 2026 (after market close on Bourse de Montciel, Segment Réglementé) together with preliminary unaudited Q2 revenue variance >12% versus guidance constitute information privilégiée under Art 7 MAR; we propose a delay under Art 17(4) MAR until SPA signing, maintain and update insider lists (Art 18 MAR), and prepare a communiqué de presse for immediate release if a leak is suspected.
Selective disclosure is prohibited (Art 10 MAR); any market sounding with financial institutions must follow Art 11 MAR with prior wall-crossing notices and recorded consents, and no pre-briefing of analysts or key investors is permitted before public dissemination via the OAM Montciel

must_detect.matched_text:
Subject: MAR, GDPR (incl. Art 9), cross-border transfer, and CSRD/SFDR workflows for Q2 trading update and contemplated acquisition of Heliovia Wind GmbH (private).
The contemplated binding SPA targeted for 14 June 2026 (after market close on Bourse de Montciel, Segment Réglementé) together with preliminary unaudited Q2 revenue variance >12% versus guidance constitute information privilégiée under Art 7 MAR; we propose a delay under Art 17(4) MAR until SPA signing, maintain and update insider lists (Art 18 MAR), and prepare a communiqué de presse for immediate release if a leak is suspected.
Selective disclosure is prohibited (Art 10 MAR); any market sounding with financial institutions must follow Art 11 MAR with prior wall-crossing notices and recorded consents, and no pre-briefing of analysts or key investors is permitted before public dissemination via the OAM Montciel.
Closed period under Art 19(11) MAR begins 30 days before H1 results (H1 publication scheduled 24 July 2026), so all PDMRs (including Mme Dr Élise Montreux, CFO, and M. Paolo Virel, CEO) are restricted from trading, and any exceptional clearance requests must be documented and justified.
GDPR: the due diligence dataroom contains employee payroll, trade union membership indicators, and limited occupational health data; these are special categories (Art 9 GDPR) and must be minimized, accessed on a need-to-know basis, with a documented DPIA, and processed under Art 9(2)(f) and, where applicable, Art 9(2)(b) with appropriate safeguards.
Cross-border data transfer: reviewers in our Norlandia subsidiary (non-EEA) require access; implement 2021 SCCs, complete a Transfer Impact Assessment, pseudonymize HR data where feasible, and restrict downloads; processors must be bound by EU-standard confidentiality and breach-notification terms.
CSRD: ongoing double materiality assessment must not be selectively disclosed; avoid forward-looking claims (e.g., “taxonomy-aligned CapEx rising to 42%”) before public reporting, and ensure SFDR PAI datapacks for investors are distributed only after publication through the OAM and on our website.
Public-status note: the communiqué de presse dated 7 May 2026 on Q1 production metrics, disseminated via OAM Montciel, and the 2025 Document d’Enregistrement Universel filed with the Autorité des Marchés Continentaux (AMC) on 30 April 2026 are already public and may be referenced in investor Q&A.
If we observe abnormal price/volume suggesting a leak, we will publish without undue delay (Art 17(1) MAR); for any continued delay, keep a written rationale and evidence that confidentiality is preserved and the public is not misled, and notify the AMC after disclosure as required by Art 17(4).
Insider list management: include all PDMRs, Strategy M&A, Treasury, external counsel Veridian Partners SCP, and distribution agent NovaEQS Dissemination SARL; obtain acknowledgments, keep timestamps, and store the list in the AMC-compliant template.
Non-issuer operations, such as the cafeteria vending machine swap next week and the office Wi‑Fi upgrade, are not relevant to MAR and should not be treated as inside information.
Communications: all investor interactions must cite only regulated information; any slide decks require Compliance clearance and should be released after market close with simultaneous OAM filing, aligning with Bourse de Montciel timing guidance.
